questions.prompt: What is the fundamental economic problem that every society must address?
choices: Scarcity of resources relative to unlimited wants
questions.correctText: Scarcity of resources relative to unlimited wants

questions.prompt: Opportunity cost is best defined as:
choices: The dollar price paid for a good
The value of the next best alternative given up
The tax imposed on a purchase
The profit earned by a business
questions.correctText: The value of the next best alternative given up

questions.prompt: A point inside a production possibilities frontier (PPF) indicates:
questions.correctText: Underutilization of resources

questions.prompt: Which economic system relies on tradition and customs to answer the basic economic questions?
questions.correctText: Traditional economy

questions.prompt: In a pure command economy, decisions about production and distribution are made by:
questions.correctText: The central government

questions.prompt: Which philosopher's 1776 work 'The Wealth of Nations' introduced the concept of the 'invisible hand'?
questions.correctText: Adam Smith

questions.prompt: The law of demand states that as the price of a good rises:
questions.correctText: Quantity demanded falls

questions.prompt: The law of supply states that as price increases:
questions.correctText: Quantity supplied increases

questions.prompt: An increase in consumer income would most likely cause the demand curve for a normal good to:
questions.correctText: Shift to the right

questions.prompt: Coffee and tea are substitutes. If the price of coffee rises sharply, the demand for tea will:
questions.correctText: Increase

questions.prompt: Peanut butter and jelly are complements. A sharp rise in the price of peanut butter would cause demand for jelly to:
questions.correctText: Decrease

questions.prompt: A new tax on producers of a good will most likely:
choices: Shift the supply curve to the right
Shift the supply curve to the left
Shift the demand curve to the right
Cause a movement along the supply curve
questions.correctText: Shift the supply curve to the left

questions.prompt: An improvement in production technology will most likely:
questions.correctText: Increase supply

questions.prompt: At the market equilibrium price:
questions.correctText: Quantity demanded equals quantity supplied

questions.prompt: If the market price is above equilibrium, the result is a:
questions.correctText: Surplus

questions.prompt: If the market price is below equilibrium, the result is a:
questions.correctText: Shortage

questions.prompt: A price ceiling set below the equilibrium price typically causes:
questions.correctText: A shortage

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the most common real-world example of a price ceiling?
questions.correctText: Rent control

questions.prompt: A binding minimum wage set above equilibrium is an example of a price floor that typically causes:
questions.correctText: A labor surplus (unemployment)

questions.prompt: Demand is said to be elastic when:
choices: A small change in price causes a large change in quantity demanded
A large change in price causes a small change in quantity demanded
Quantity does not respond to price
questions.correctText: A small change in price causes a large change in quantity demanded

questions.prompt: Which of the following is most likely to have inelastic demand?
questions.correctText: Insulin for diabetics

questions.prompt: Which factor tends to make demand more elastic?
choices: It takes a small share of income
questions.correctText: Many available substitutes

questions.prompt: Consumer surplus is best defined as:
choices: The difference between what consumers pay and what they were willing to pay
The total amount spent by consumers
questions.correctText: The difference between what consumers pay and what they were willing to pay

questions.prompt: Deadweight loss refers to:
choices: The reduction in total surplus caused by market inefficiency
questions.correctText: The reduction in total surplus caused by market inefficiency

questions.prompt: In perfect competition, an individual firm is a:
questions.correctText: Price taker

questions.prompt: Which characteristic defines a monopoly market?
choices: One seller with significant barriers to entry
questions.correctText: One seller with significant barriers to entry

questions.prompt: Which is the best example of monopolistic competition?
questions.correctText: Local restaurants and clothing stores

questions.prompt: Oligopoly markets are characterized by:
choices: A few large firms whose decisions are interdependent
questions.correctText: A few large firms whose decisions are interdependent

questions.prompt: Which industry is the clearest example of an oligopoly?
questions.correctText: Commercial airlines

questions.prompt: The Sherman Antitrust Act of 1890 was designed to:
choices: Outlaw monopolies and restraints of trade
questions.correctText: Outlaw monopolies and restraints of trade

questions.prompt: Which federal agency, created in 1914, enforces antitrust and consumer protection laws?
questions.correctText: FTC

questions.prompt: The Clayton Antitrust Act of 1914 specifically targeted:
choices: Price discrimination, tying, and mergers that reduce competition
questions.correctText: Price discrimination, tying, and mergers that reduce competition

questions.prompt: In a competitive labor market, wages are determined primarily by:
questions.correctText: Supply and demand for labor

questions.prompt: A labor union's primary economic function is to:
choices: Negotiate collectively for higher wages and better conditions
Increase the supply of unskilled labor
questions.correctText: Negotiate collectively for higher wages and better conditions

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a negative externality?
choices: A vaccinated person reducing disease spread
questions.correctText: A factory polluting a river

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a positive externality?
questions.correctText: Vaccination reducing community disease

questions.prompt: Public goods such as national defense are best described as:
questions.correctText: Non-rival and non-excludable

questions.prompt: The 'tragedy of the commons' refers to:
choices: Overuse of a shared resource because no one owns it
questions.correctText: Overuse of a shared resource because no one owns it

questions.prompt: A Pigouvian tax is designed to:
choices: Correct a negative externality by making polluters pay
Lower the price of luxury goods
questions.correctText: Correct a negative externality by making polluters pay

questions.prompt: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is defined as the total market value of:
choices: All final goods and services produced in a country in a year
All goods ever produced by a country
All stocks traded in a year
questions.correctText: All final goods and services produced in a country in a year

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a component of GDP using the expenditure approach (C + I + G + NX)?
questions.correctText: Stock market gains

questions.prompt: Real GDP differs from nominal GDP because real GDP:
questions.correctText: Is adjusted for inflation

questions.prompt: GDP per capita is most useful for comparing:
choices: Average standard of living between countries
questions.correctText: Average standard of living between countries

questions.prompt: The four phases of the business cycle in order are:
questions.correctText: Expansion, peak, recession, trough

questions.prompt: A recession is commonly defined as:
choices: A single quarter of slow growth
Two consecutive quarters of declining real GDP
questions.correctText: Two consecutive quarters of declining real GDP

questions.prompt: An unemployed worker who lost a job to automation is classified as:
questions.correctText: Structurally unemployed

questions.prompt: A recent college graduate searching for her first job is best classified as:
questions.correctText: Frictionally unemployed

questions.prompt: Cyclical unemployment is caused primarily by:
questions.correctText: Downturns in the business cycle

questions.prompt: Inflation is most commonly measured in the United States by the:
questions.correctText: Consumer Price Index (CPI)

questions.prompt: Stagflation refers to the simultaneous occurrence of:
choices: High inflation, high unemployment, and stagnant growth
questions.correctText: High inflation, high unemployment, and stagnant growth

questions.prompt: Deflation is defined as:
choices: A general decrease in the price level
A drop in the stock market
questions.correctText: A general decrease in the price level

questions.prompt: Fiscal policy is conducted by:
choices: Congress and the President through taxing and spending
questions.correctText: Congress and the President through taxing and spending

questions.prompt: Which would be an example of expansionary fiscal policy?
choices: Selling government bonds to the public
questions.correctText: Increasing federal spending on infrastructure

questions.prompt: Contractionary fiscal policy might involve:
questions.correctText: Tax increases and reduced spending

questions.prompt: A budget deficit occurs when:
choices: Government spending exceeds tax revenue in a year
questions.correctText: Government spending exceeds tax revenue in a year

questions.prompt: The national debt is best described as:
choices: The accumulated total of past deficits minus surpluses
questions.correctText: The accumulated total of past deficits minus surpluses

questions.prompt: A progressive tax is one in which:
choices: Everyone pays the same dollar amount
The tax rate rises as income rises
The tax rate falls as income rises
questions.correctText: The tax rate rises as income rises

questions.prompt: A regressive tax takes:
choices: A larger share of income from low-income earners than from high-income earners
A larger share from high earners
questions.correctText: A larger share of income from low-income earners than from high-income earners

questions.prompt: The Laffer curve illustrates the idea that:
choices: Tax revenue always rises with higher tax rates
Beyond a certain point, higher tax rates can reduce total tax revenue
questions.correctText: Beyond a certain point, higher tax rates can reduce total tax revenue

questions.prompt: Monetary policy in the United States is conducted by:
questions.correctText: The Federal Reserve System

questions.prompt: The committee within the Fed that sets monetary policy through open market operations is the:
questions.correctText: Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC)

questions.prompt: When the Fed buys government bonds in the open market, the money supply generally:
questions.correctText: Increases

questions.prompt: Which is NOT one of the Federal Reserve's traditional policy tools?
questions.correctText: Setting income tax rates

questions.prompt: The discount rate is the interest rate the Fed charges:
choices: Commercial banks borrowing directly from the Fed
questions.correctText: Commercial banks borrowing directly from the Fed

questions.prompt: Quantitative easing (QE) refers to the Fed's policy of:
choices: Large-scale purchases of long-term assets to lower long-term interest rates
questions.correctText: Large-scale purchases of long-term assets to lower long-term interest rates

questions.prompt: M1 money supply includes:
choices: Currency in circulation and checkable deposits
questions.correctText: Currency in circulation and checkable deposits

questions.prompt: Fiat money has value primarily because:
choices: Government has declared it legal tender and people accept it
questions.correctText: Government has declared it legal tender and people accept it

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT one of the three basic functions of money?
questions.correctText: Source of consumption

questions.prompt: Fractional reserve banking means that banks:
choices: Lend out a portion of deposits while keeping a fraction in reserves
Are required to invest in gold
questions.correctText: Lend out a portion of deposits while keeping a fraction in reserves

questions.prompt: If the reserve requirement is 10%, the simple money multiplier is:
Comparative advantage, developed by David Ricardo, states that a country should specialize in goods it can produce at:
choices: A lower opportunity cost than its trading partners
The same cost as everyone else
questions.correctText: A lower opportunity cost than its trading partners

questions.prompt: Absolute advantage refers to a country's ability to:
choices: Produce more of a good with the same resources than another country
questions.correctText: Produce more of a good with the same resources than another country

questions.prompt: A tariff is best defined as:
questions.correctText: A tax on imported goods

questions.prompt: A quota is:
choices: A physical limit on the quantity of imports
questions.correctText: A physical limit on the quantity of imports

questions.prompt: A country has a trade deficit when:
questions.correctText: Imports exceed exports

questions.prompt: An exchange rate is best defined as:
choices: The price of one country's currency in terms of another's
The interest rate set by the Fed
questions.correctText: The price of one country's currency in terms of another's

questions.prompt: Which agreement, signed in 1994 and replaced in 2020 by USMCA, eliminated most tariffs among the U.S., Canada, and Mexico?
questions.correctText: NAFTA

questions.prompt: The WTO's primary purpose is to:
choices: Promote and regulate international trade among member nations
questions.correctText: Promote and regulate international trade among member nations

questions.prompt: The European Union is best described as:
choices: An economic and political union of European countries, many sharing the euro
A free-trade zone among all Asian nations
A subsidiary of the United Nations
questions.correctText: An economic and political union of European countries, many sharing the euro

questions.prompt: Protectionists generally argue for tariffs to:
choices: Protect domestic industries and jobs from foreign competition
questions.correctText: Protect domestic industries and jobs from foreign competition

questions.prompt: John Maynard Keynes's 1936 'General Theory' argued that during recessions, governments should:
choices: Increase aggregate demand through fiscal stimulus
questions.correctText: Increase aggregate demand through fiscal stimulus

questions.prompt: Monetarism, associated with Milton Friedman, emphasizes controlling:
choices: The growth rate of the money supply
questions.correctText: The growth rate of the money supply

questions.prompt: Supply-side economics emphasizes that economic growth is best achieved by:
choices: Lowering taxes and reducing regulation to encourage production
questions.correctText: Lowering taxes and reducing regulation to encourage production

questions.prompt: Which of the following is widely identified as a cause of the Great Depression that began in 1929?
choices: A stock market crash, bank failures, and a contraction of the money supply
questions.correctText: A stock market crash, bank failures, and a contraction of the money supply

questions.prompt: Franklin Roosevelt's New Deal response to the Great Depression is most consistent with which school of thought?
questions.correctText: Keynesian demand-side economics

questions.prompt: Stagflation in the 1970s was triggered in part by:
choices: OPEC oil shocks combined with loose monetary policy
questions.correctText: OPEC oil shocks combined with loose monetary policy

questions.prompt: OPEC is best described as:
choices: A cartel of major oil-exporting countries
A regulatory agency of the EU
questions.correctText: A cartel of major oil-exporting countries

questions.prompt: The 2008 financial crisis was triggered most directly by:
choices: A collapse in subprime mortgage lending and related securities
questions.correctText: A collapse in subprime mortgage lending and related securities

questions.prompt: TARP, passed in 2008, was a program designed to:
choices: Purchase troubled assets and stabilize banks
questions.correctText: Purchase troubled assets and stabilize banks

questions.prompt: The 2008 collapse of which investment bank intensified the financial crisis?
questions.correctText: Lehman Brothers

questions.prompt: A major economic feature of the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020-2021 was:
choices: Massive supply chain disruption and unprecedented fiscal/monetary stimulus
questions.correctText: Massive supply chain disruption and unprecedented fiscal/monetary stimulus

questions.prompt: A personal budget is a plan that compares:
choices: Income to expenses over a given period
questions.correctText: Income to expenses over a given period

questions.prompt: The Annual Percentage Rate (APR) on a credit card represents the:
choices: Monthly fee charged by a bank
Annualized cost of borrowing including interest and certain fees
questions.correctText: Annualized cost of borrowing including interest and certain fees

questions.prompt: Compound interest differs from simple interest because compound interest is calculated on:
choices: The principal plus any accumulated interest
questions.correctText: The principal plus any accumulated interest

questions.prompt: A checking account is generally used for:
questions.correctText: Frequent transactions and bill payments

questions.prompt: Which type of investment represents ownership in a corporation?
questions.correctText: Stocks

questions.prompt: A bond is best described as:
choices: A loan from an investor to a corporation or government
questions.correctText: A loan from an investor to a corporation or government

questions.prompt: Diversification reduces investment risk by:
choices: Putting all funds into one stock
questions.correctText: Spreading investments across different assets

questions.prompt: Marginal thinking in economics involves:
choices: Evaluating the additional benefit and additional cost of one more unit
questions.correctText: Evaluating the additional benefit and additional cost of one more unit

questions.prompt: The U.S. economy is best described as a:
choices: Mixed economy with private markets and government regulation
questions.correctText: Mixed economy with private markets and government regulation
